data.content:
Implementation plan:
1. Extend `src/lib/beat-state-overview.ts` sizing helpers so width calculation accepts a sizing column count separate from the currently rendered count, caps width at availableWidth / 6, and exposes a pure watermark transition helper.
2. Update `BeatStateOverview` to keep per-tab sizing watermarks in component state. For each active tab, start from visible column count, then when visible columns exceed the current watermark promote the watermark to visible + 2 so shrink pressure has headroom; when visible columns decrease, keep the existing watermark.
3. Keep hidden empty columns exactly as shipped: render only `visibleOverviewGroups(groups)` and show compact empty state for empty tabs.
4. Add focused unit tests for max one-sixth width, two-column pressure promotion, no-grow behavior after decreases, and tab-local independence.
5. Verify in browser: desktop/mobile overflow, current All Repositories overview column widths, and tab switching.

Complexity: low to medium; the main risk is a state update loop in the React sizing watermark effect. Keep the watermark update pure and only return a new object when the active tab value changes.

Invariants: none listed; existing read-only overview behavior and repo/id display rules must remain unchanged.
